titleStmt.title: SII 28.174: original edition by J. Sundaram
No. 174. (A.R. No. 329 of 1913.) CHIDAMBARAM, CHIDAMBARAM TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.

summary.p: Naṭarāja temple, base of the Nandi-maṇḍapa opposite to the southern gōpuram

This record in Tamil verse tells about the victory gained by Muṉaiyāvalliyādittaṉ of Kāmapeṟu, over Vaḷavaṉ (Chōḻa) for the sake of saving the honour of Mīṉavar (Pāṇḍya) and his capturing of elephants, horses, the anklet of heroes, chowries, conches and lastly of the village Paṉaiyūr. These verses were composed by Tāyi[lu*]nalla-perumāḷ Muṉaiyadaraiyan alias Bhuvanēkavīra-Toṇḍaimāṉār. The vanquished Poṉṉināḍuḍaiyāṉ is stated to have looked for as opportunity to request for the restoration of his crown and (by granting that request) the Toṇḍai-Ādimuṉaiyaṉ could claim to have been a bestower of crowns. The hero of these verses is referred to as Toṇḍaimāṉ Rāchchūriya (Rāja-Sūrya) Maṉṉavaṉ in the last verse.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
vārittalam pukaḻ miṉavaṟkāka vaḷavaṉai veṉṟārkkāmapeṟṟiṉar muṉaiyāvalliyātittaṉē virakkaḻaluṅ kavariyum veḻamum vempariyum muriccura caṅkamum paṉaiyūrumuramurame § . tolliyāṉ ceṉṟu matiṟ kūṭalāṉai Aḻikkavalliyāṉ cettoruvalla paṅkeḷ Ellaimuḻutum pukaḻumuṉaiyaṉātittaṉ vaḻuti māṉaṅkāttāṉ vantu § veṉṟi puṉaimuṉaiyaṉ veṉṟāṉ paṉaicaiyarkoṉ maṉṟuḷāṉaṟiya māṟṟalarai ceṉṟu porupaṉaiṅkāṭeṅku muṭip poṟpiṉaiyal tūkkap peruṅkāṭāyiṉaveye § veṉṟāṉmuṉaiyaṉ virutācaraik kaḷattiṟ koṉṟāṉeṉa vaḻuti cerapāṭi maṉṟe vaṭavaracar taṅkaḷ maṇavāḷaṉeṉṟāṟ kaṭakañ ceṟttāṉiraṇṭu kaikku §
tāyi nallaperumāḷ muṉaiyatarai Āna puvanekavira toṇṭaimāṉārkavi § koṭi veṇṭiya toṅkal kārtten Eṉṟu kūṟum vaṇṭe Aṭi veṇṭimāviviyāṟ ceṉṟatāyin Alavaḻuti paṭi veṇṭavum poṉṉināṭuṭaiyaṉ yiṭam pārttu niṉṟu muṭiveṇṭavum vaḻaṅkum toṇṭai Ātimuṉaiyaṉukke §
piramapuṟṟa tāvaṭam poṇañcaṭṭai piṟaṅku caṅkaṅkaram peṟṟa coṭṭaiyu payaviḷakkuṭaṉ kaṇkaḷorāyiram peṟṟa Intiraṉ poṟṟillaiyicaṉaiyetta muṉṉāḷ varam peṟṟavaṉ toṇṭaimāṉ rācacūriya maṉṉavaṉe §
Digital edition of SII 28.174 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
